title: adenocarpus viscosus subsp. spartioides
intro: Adenocarpus viscosus subsp. spartioides, also known as Sticky Adenocarpus or Greek broom, is a plant species native to the Mediterranean region. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic effects, which are attributed to its saponin and flavonoid content. The plant is valued for its health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, relieving digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. The herbal preparations of Adenocarpus viscosus subsp. spartioides include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, salves, and creams to harness its medicinal properties.
